action.hypothesis: `lowrank_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual` has now beaten the `K=1` anchor, so the next question is whether the same hybrid can also improve `K=2` and begin subsuming the current `lowrank_soft_codebook_residual` low-K band.
action.summary: Run one controlled architecture comparison at `K=2` with `lowrank_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual`, keeping `EF=12`, `signum`, `LR=3.2e-3`, and `AUXK_ALPHA=0.0` fixed so the only changed dimension relative to the current `K=2` frontier point is architecture.
action.self_review: This is the cleanest follow-up after a new-family win at the endpoint. It tests whether the hybrid is just a `K=1` specialist or the start of a broader replacement for the existing low-K winner, without mixing in any recipe drift.
notes_to_memory: Round 71 tests whether `lowrank_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual`, newly on the frontier at `K=1`, can also improve the `K=2` slot under the same fixed recipe.
Keep architecture and recipe fixed (`EF=12`, `signum`, `LR=3.2e-3`, `AUXK_ALPHA=0.0`) so this is a clean same-`K` architecture comparison against the current `K=2` frontier point.

Success criterion is beating the current `K=2` frontier anchor (`lowrank_soft_codebook_residual`, FVU `0.16154605150222778`) or at least establishing the hybrid as clearly competitive beyond the endpoint.
next_hypotheses: If `K=2` wins, test `K=4` to map whether the hybrid can replace `lowrank_soft_codebook_residual` across more of the low-K frontier.
If `K=2` is close but slightly behind, consider one confirmatory rerun before treating the hybrid as a `K=1` specialist.
If `K=2` clearly loses, retain the hybrid as the `K=1` endpoint winner and keep `lowrank_soft_codebook_residual` for `K>=2`.

result.description: Run one controlled architecture comparison at `K=2` with `lowrank_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual`, keeping `EF=12`, `signum`, `LR=3.2e-3`, and `AUXK_ALPHA=0.0` fixed so the only changed dimension relative to the current `K=2` frontier point is architecture.
result.self_review: This is the cleanest follow-up after a new-family win at the endpoint. It tests whether the hybrid is just a `K=1` specialist or the start of a broader replacement for the existing low-K winner, without mixing in any recipe drift.
